steps.title: Identify the shared critique
steps.content: Both texts argue that scholars have overly focused on whether modernist fiction is fragmented or coherent, neglecting other stylistic elements and giving a distorted view of the period.

steps.title: Apply the critique to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B states that without a major shift in focus, the presented vision will remain unnecessarily limited, exactly reflecting the authors’ warning that the current narrow focus distorts understanding.
The other three either suggest widening focus beyond style, claim methods are obsolete, or propose rehabilitating reputations—none capture the shared concern about the fragmentation‑versus‑coherence bias limiting the vision.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
